The Light Between Oceans (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $12,545,979Details
International Box Office $9,735,753
Worldwide Box Office $22,281,732
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,245,364
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $702,986
Total $1,948,350
Further financial details...

Synopsis

On a remote Australian island in the years following World War I, lighthouse keeper Tom Sherbourne and his wife, Isabel, discover a boat washed ashore carrying a dead man and a two-month old baby. Rescuing the infant, they make the decision to raise her as their own but the consequences of their choice prove to be devastating.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$4,765,838 (38.0% of total gross)
Legs:2.63 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:56.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:1,500 opening theaters/1,500 max. theaters, 3.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $17,346,808

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 2nd, 2016 (Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: September 2nd, 2016 (Wide), released as Hayat Işığım (Turkey)
September 9th, 2016 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
September 9th, 2016 (Wide) (Germany)
September 9th, 2016 (Wide) (Argentina)
September 22nd, 2016 (Wide), released as La Luz Entre Los Océanos (Mexico)
Video Release: January 24th, 2017 by Dreamworks Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic material and some sexual content.
(Rating bulletin 2387, 8/19/2015)
Running Time: 132 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Epilogue
Geographical setting: Australia
Plot point: Death of a Spouse or Fiancée / Fiancé, Missing Child, Romance, Unexpected Families
Social setting: Adopted Family
Subgenre: Family Drama
Time period setting: 1910s, 1920s, 1950s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Heyday Films, Entertainment One, DreamWorks Pictures, Reliance Entertainment, Participant Media
Production Countries: United Kingdom, United States, Australia, New Zealand, India
Languages: English

Synopsis

On a remote Australian island in the years following World War I, lighthouse keeper Tom Sherbourne and his wife, Isabel, discover a boat washed ashore carrying a dead man and a two-month old baby. Rescuing the infant, they make the decision to raise her as their own but the consequences of their choice prove to be devastating.
